differentials.name: Avoid fluid overload
differentials.raw: Avoid fluid overload (NOT to increase shunt volume)

differentials.name: Antiprostaglandins = indomethacin opposes prostaglandins, which are potent duct dilators (b)  Surgical ligation
differentials.raw: Antiprostaglandins = indomethacin opposes prostaglandins, which are potent duct dilators (b)  Surgical ligation

Antiprostaglandins = indomethacin oppose: premature infant not subject to medial muscular hypertrophy of small pulmonary artery branches (which occurs in normal infants subsequent to progressive hypoxia in 3rd trimester) Cause: (a)  pulmonary artery pressure remains low without opposing any L-to-R shunts (PDA / VSD) (b)  ductus arteriosus remains open ← hypoxia in RDS

context: premature infant not subject to medial muscular hypertrophy of small pulmonary artery branches (which occurs in normal infants subsequent to progressive hypoxia in 3rd trimester) Cause: (a)  pulmonary artery pressure remains low without opposing any L-to-R shunts (PDA / VSD) (b)  ductus arteriosus remains open ← hypoxia in RDS √  recurrence of alveolar airspace filling after resolution of RDS √  granular pattern of hyaline membrane disease becomes more opaque √  enlargement of heart (masked by positive pressure ventilation) Rx: (a)  Medical therapy:
